name: spec-workflow
description: Spec-driven autonomous build workflow: a GitHub Project board is the source of truth, tasks are implemented TDD-first by delegated dev agents, a single quality gate decides advancement, and humans steer asynchronously. Per-repo config in .claude/project.yaml (schemaVersion 2).

interface.shortDescription: Board-driven autonomous TDD build loop with delegated dev and reviewer agents.
interface.longDescription: Spec Workflow turns a GitHub Project board into the single source of truth for building software. It picks the next task honoring priority, epic sequencing, dependency guards, and a WIP limit; implements it test-first through delegated dev subagents; enforces a mandatory quality gate; and runs a two-pass review (spec compliance then code quality) before opening a PR or merging. Humans steer asynchronously through issue comments and UI-option pages, while checkpoint/handoff support pauses and resumes the loop at safe boundaries. Everything project-specific lives in the consumer repo's .claude/project.yaml.
defaultPrompt: Set up this repository for the spec-workflow and seed the board from the spec.
Work the backlog: pick the next task and implement it test-first to a green gate.
What should I work on next on the board?
